content:
With a few small refinements, your materials will be even stronger. Keep up the great work!

Best,
Professor Padavano

Grades:
• Cover Letter: 80/100
• Resume: 85/100



⸻




Some of the previously uploaded files have expired. If you need me to review any of those again, please re-upload them. Meanwhile, I’ll proceed with evaluating this final batch of resume and cover letter pairs and provide feedback in the same workshop-style format. Stay tuned!

Feedback Letters for the Final Batch of Students

⸻

Letter for Justin Kim

Dear Justin,

Thank you for submitting your resume and cover letter. You’ve done a great job presenting your skills and experience professionally. Below is my feedback.

Your cover letter is well-structured and effectively highlights your qualifications. One area where you could improve is making the introduction more engaging—starting with a compelling hook or a specific reason why you’re drawn to this company would strengthen your opening. Additionally, you provide a good summary of your skills, but incorporating specific achievements or projects that demonstrate those skills would make your letter even stronger. Your closing paragraph is polite and professional, but a stronger call to action (such as expressing eagerness for an interview) would leave a more lasting impression.
